title: Zoneless Angular
context: Angular 21 provides stable zoneless change detection via `provideZonelessChangeDetection()`. Zone.js was a monkey-patching layer that intercepted async operations to trigger change detection — powerful but opaque, expensive (~15KB), and unnecessary when all components use OnPush + signals.
Actions is a greenfield Angular 21 project. All components will use standalone components, OnPush change detection, and signal-based state from day one.
decision: **Remove Zone.js entirely.** Use `provideZonelessChangeDetection()` in the application bootstrap.
- Side effects use `effect()` sparingly (prefer computed signals)
- No `NgZone` injection anywhere in the codebase
consequences: - Change detection only runs when signals change — no unnecessary cycles
- Removes an entire layer of framework complexity
- Aligns with Angular's declared future direction
- Third-party libraries that depend on Zone.js patching may not trigger change detection automatically (verify AG Grid Enterprise 35 and ng-bootstrap compatibility)

**Mitigation:** Bootstrap 5 is CSS-only (no Zone.js dependency). AG Grid Enterprise 35 supports zoneless mode. ng-bootstrap: to be verified during initial integration — if incompatible, evaluate alternatives (direct Bootstrap 5 usage or Zone.js polyfill for specific components).

**Relationship to ADR-0006:** ADR-0006 establishes signals as the state primitive. This ADR removes Zone.js, which is safe only because ADR-0006 ensures no component relies on zone-triggered change detection.
origin: Identity decision (ADR-0012), adopted by Actions as non-negotiable for Angular 21 greenfield.
